title: Head-direction cells as a neural compass in bats navigating outdoors on a remote oceanic island.

assets.abstract: Animals and humans rely on their navigation skills to survive. However, spatial neurons in the brain's "navigation circuit" had not previously been studied under real-world conditions. We conducted an electrophysiological study of spatial neurons in the wild: We recorded head-direction cells from the presubiculum of bats flying unconstrained and navigating outdoors on a remote oceanic island. These neurons represented the bats' orientation stably across the island's entire geographical scale and

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Visionary cosmologies — self-transcendence, mystical experience, numinous, awe, cosmic consciousness.
